hero.eyebrow: Hotel back-office software · works alongside any PMS
hero.headline: Everything your PMS doesn't do.
hero.sub: MyHotelOps is the operations layer that runs alongside your hotel's reservation system. Maintenance, events, vendors, signage, guest arrival — every part of running a boutique hotel that lives outside your PMS, in one place.
hero.personaLine: Built for owners, GMs, and managers — not for line staff fighting your reservation system.
hero.trialLine: 7-day free trial. No credit card. 10 GB included.

positioning.title: The category we're not in: your PMS.
positioning.body: We will never replace Cloudbeds, Mews, Opera, or Little Hotelier. We sit next to them and handle every operational surface they leave to spreadsheets and group chats — work orders, event proposals, vendor logins, signage on every TV, the printable arrival card in every room.

footer.tagline: The operations layer for boutique hotels.

about.headline: Boutique hotels deserve software built for them.
about.missionBody: Big PMS suites and big-chain tooling were never built for the 20-, 40-, 60-room boutique. They bundle modules nobody asked for at prices that don't fit, then leave the actual day-to-day operations to spreadsheets, group chats, and paper. We think that's backwards. The boutique market is underserved by software, and the people running these properties — the GMs, the owners, the managers who actually live the chaos — deserve tools that fit their reality.
about.sectionTwoBody: MyHotelOps is the operations layer that runs alongside whatever PMS you already use. We handle the things your reservation system doesn't — maintenance work orders, event proposals, vendor logins, signage on every TV, the printable arrival card in every room. One subscription per property, no per-seat games, no per-screen tax. The kind of pricing a small business can plan around.
about.sectionThreeBody: Independent boutique hotels and small groups, anywhere in the world — the US, Mexico, Europe, Asia. People who care about hospitality as a craft and want their software to do the same. If that's you, we built this for you.

blog.headline: The state of boutique hotel operations.
blog.sub: Notes on the underserved corner of hospitality tech — realistic operations budgets, the modernization gap, what 2026 guests now expect, and how independent properties can catch up without enterprise spend.
blog.ctaHeadline: See it on your own property.
blog.ctaSub: 7-day free trial. No credit card. Full access to maintenance, events, signage, and arrival.

features.title: Built like the boutique back office actually works.
features.sub: The full surface area, in one place. No per-module pricing — every feature below is in the $100/property base or one of the three flat-rate add-ons. Nothing here costs extra by seat, by screen, or by room.

items.name: Work Orders
items.description: Photo-first maintenance tickets on a Kanban board. Every status change is time-stamped, every fix has visual evidence in the audit log.

items.name: Events & Catering
items.description: Inquiry → proposal → invoice in one place. Send a branded PDF in minutes; track payments without a separate billing tool.

items.name: IT Hub
items.description: Wi-Fi credentials, vendor logins, equipment serial numbers, warranty dates — searchable, role-gated, one source of truth.

items.name: Vendor Directory
items.description: Plumbers, electricians, linen suppliers, food vendors — names, contacts, contracts, and last-called dates in one Rolodex.

items.name: Branded Arrival Page
items.description: Per-room QR card that opens to your hotel's brand, your Wi-Fi, your room-service menus. No guest account, no app to download.

items.name: Digital Signage
items.description: Any browser-capable TV becomes a display. Schedule by day, hour, or zone. Three screens included; unlimited via the add-on.

items.name: Emergency Broadcast
items.description: Take over every screen at a property in one click. Pre-built templates for fire, weather, evacuation; custom messages anytime.

items.name: Guest Issue Intake
items.description: Guests scan a QR and report problems in their language. Tickets land in the same Kanban board as staff-reported work orders.

items.name: Global Media CDN
items.description: Photos and videos served from the edge worldwide. Same fast load in Tokyo, Lisbon, or Toronto. 25 GB included on every plan.

items.name: Floor Plans & Documents
items.description: One canonical place for layouts, equipment manuals, vendor warranties, brand guidelines. Versioned, downloadable, never lost in chat.

items.name: Branded Text Cards
items.description: Build sign content in-app with your brand color and typography. No Canva, no PowerPoint round-trip.

items.name: Restaurant & Room Service Menus
items.description: Editable menus that auto-publish to the arrival page and signage. Update once, every surface refreshes.

items.name: Social Studio (add-on)
items.description: One AI-drafted social post per property per day — captions, hashtags, and a photo picked from this catalog. Copy, download, or email it to yourself; you publish from your phone. No platform integrations.

items.name: Unlimited Team Members
items.description: Front desk, housekeeping, engineering, ownership — every staff member at every property included, no per-seat charges, ever.

items.name: Role-based Access
items.description: Owners, GMs, managers, line staff — each role sees only what they need. Granular permissions per property, per module.

items.name: Multi-property Console
items.description: Switch between properties in one click. Same login, same workflows. Group-level dashboards roll up automatically.

items.name: Two-factor Authentication
items.description: Optional 2FA per user via any authenticator app. Owners can require it org-wide; recovery flow handled through admin support.

items.name: Per-property Pricing
items.description: No per-seat, no per-screen, no per-room math. One flat number per property, predictable forever.

items.name: USD Billing, Worldwide Cards
items.description: One simple billing currency: US Dollars. Any major credit card works from anywhere — your bank handles the FX conversion. Stripe Tax adds the correct local VAT / GST per your country.

items.name: No Contracts
items.description: Cancel from your account any time. No annual commitments, no notice periods, no exit interviews.

items.name: 7-day Free Trial
items.description: Full feature access for 7 days. No credit card to start; your data persists if you convert and exports if you don't.

destinations.title: Made for independent and small-group hotels everywhere.
destinations.sub: Lisbon, Barcelona, Paris, Mexico City — the neighborhoods where independent and small-group hotels actually run. Get started in your language and use the same operational stack everywhere. Billed in USD, and most major credit cards are supported.

asianDestinations.title: Built for boutique hotels across Asia, too.
asianDestinations.sub: Tokyo, Seoul, Hanoi, Singapore — from design hotels and ryokans to lifestyle properties and small luxury groups. Get started in your language and run the same operational stack we support for boutiques in Europe and the Americas. Billed in USD, and most major credit cards are supported.

items.blurb: Shibuya design hotels, Asakusa ryokans, Ginza lifestyle properties — 日本語 signup, the same maintenance + events + signage stack.
Gangnam boutiques, Itaewon hostels, Jeju resort branches — 한국어 signup, one subscription per property.
Old Quarter boutiques, Hội An heritage hotels, Da Nang beach properties — Tiếng Việt signup, flat per-property pricing that scales.
Chinatown shophouse hotels, Tanjong Pagar lifestyle properties, design groups across the island — English signup, no per-screen surprises.

groups.title: One stack, every city you operate in.
groups.sub: Run a property in Lisbon, another in Mexico City, a third in Tokyo — same login, same workflows, same monthly line. Each team works in their language; owners see everything in theirs.

items.title: Local language for every team.
items.body: Staff at each property sign up and work in their language. The owner's view rolls up in theirs.

items.title: One console, every property.
items.body: Switch between cities in a click. Group dashboards aggregate automatically; per-property views stay separate when you need them.

items.title: Add or pause anytime.
items.body: Opening a new city or sunsetting a property is a few clicks — no contracts to renegotiate, no per-seat or per-screen math.

useCases.title: Three boutiques, three different daily wins.
useCases.sub: The workflows MyHotelOps was built to handle.

items.headline: Cut maintenance lag from days to hours
items.body: Front desk used to email engineering; tickets sat in inboxes. With photo-first work orders the cycle from guest report → completed fix dropped from a 2-3 day median to under 4 hours. Owner finally has a Kanban view of every open issue across the property in one place.

items.headline: Replaced four separate SaaS subscriptions
items.body: Cancelled their maintenance ticketing tool, their event-banquet platform, their digital signage subscription, their guest concierge app, and the freelance social-media manager who wrote their captions. Net spend dropped about $500/property/mo with all the same workflows still running — just consolidated under one $207 line item.

items.headline: Onboarded GM in an afternoon, not a week
items.body: New GM started on a Tuesday. By end of day they had work orders, vendor contacts, the Wi-Fi credentials, the printable arrival cards, and the events pipeline all in one tab. Owner used to budget two weeks for new-GM ramp and now allocates one afternoon.

useCases.footnote: Want to share your story? Email {email} — we'll write it up so it reads honest, not like a pitch.

demo.headline: Want a tour before you sign up?
demo.sub: If you run more than one property — or you'd rather see the product walked through than poke around the trial — book 30 minutes with us. We'll show you the parts that matter for your operation, answer the awkward billing questions, and skip the parts that don't apply.

alternative.heading: Don't need a call?
alternative.body: The 7-day free trial gives you the same access. No credit card, full feature set, your data stays if you don't convert.

pricing.headline: Per property. Not per seat. Not per screen. Not per room.
pricing.sub: One $100/property/month base. Three optional add-ons. That’s the whole price list — and the maximum a single property can possibly cost you is $207/month, regardless of how many screens, rooms, or staff you have.
pricing.midCycleNote: No contracts. No annual commitments. Add or remove options whenever you want — cancel any time from your account.

base.label: Base
base.body: Everything every property needs on day one — maintenance work orders, event proposals, IT records, media library, and 3 signage screens.

signage.label: Signage Unlimited
signage.body: Lifts the 3-screen cap. Unlimited TVs at every property — lobby, breakroom, meeting rooms, pool deck. Same scheduler, same emergency broadcast.
features: Image, video, web, or branded text card

guest.label: Guest Experience
guest.body: Branded arrival page, printable QR card for each room, and guest issue intake. Replaces $3–$6 per occupied room concierge tools at flat-rate.
features: Arrival page builder with brand color

social.label: Social Studio
social.body: An AI-drafted social post per property, every morning. Captions, hashtags, and a photo pick from your media library — ready to copy, download, or email to yourself. We never touch the social platforms.
features: One on-brand post per property per day
Image suggested from your media catalog
Thumbs up/down trains your brand voice

compare.headline: For a 40-room boutique buying these tools today.
compare.intro: Hotels typically run a maintenance ticketing tool, a digital signage subscription, and a guest concierge / arrival platform — each on a different per-screen, per-room, or per-property billing axis. Here’s what one property pays for all three à la carte versus through MyHotelOps.
compare.savings: Savings vs. standalone à la carte
compare.footnote: Standalone prices are publicly listed entry tiers as of May 2026; some vendors only quote on request and may be higher in practice. Guest-arrival vendors charge per occupied room, so the figure above assumes a 40-room property at typical mid-tier occupancy.

items.q: Is there a setup or onboarding fee?
items.a: One-time per tenant when you start with us — we apply it on the first property’s first invoice and never re-charge if you add more properties or resubscribe. We onboard your team personally; no DIY wizard.

items.q: What happens to my data if I cancel?
items.a: You keep access to your media library and IT Hub records for 30 days, with an export option on day one. After 30 days your account is closed and storage is reclaimed.

items.q: Do you charge per user or per seat?
items.a: No. Every staff member at every property is included — front desk, housekeeping, engineering, ownership. Per-property pricing is the only axis.

items.q: What if I only want maintenance, or only signage?
items.a: You can. The base subscription includes maintenance work orders, events & catering, IT Hub, media, and three signage screens at $100/property. Add Signage Unlimited, Guest Experience, or Social Studio only if you want them — toggle on /billing, billed prorated.

items.q: How is the signage add-on different from Yodeck or OptiSigns?
items.a: Flat per-property pricing — unlimited screens at a property for $49 versus $8–$30 per screen per month elsewhere. Same player concept (any browser-capable TV), same scheduling. Break-even versus Yodeck at about 6 screens; cheaper above that.

items.q: Does Social Studio post to my Instagram for me?
items.a: No, and that's by design. Social Studio drafts the post each morning — caption, hashtags, suggested photo — and lets you copy, download, or email it to yourself. You publish it from your phone. No platform integrations to break, no OAuth grants to manage, and your social accounts stay yours.

items.q: Do you integrate with our PMS (Mews, Cloudbeds, Opera)?
items.a: No, and that’s deliberate. We will never replace or sync with your PMS — we sit alongside it and handle every operational surface it leaves to spreadsheets and group chats. Reservation data stays in your PMS where it belongs; everything else (work orders, events, vendors, signage, guest arrival) lives here.

cta.headline: Start with the base. Add the rest only if you need it.
cta.sub: Sign up takes a minute. Full access for 7 days, no credit card.
